title: Vertical aquaponics system
abstract: The present disclosure relates to aquaponics and discloses an aquaponics system (100) comprising a base tank (30) supported on a floor surface; vertical elements (11) extending operatively upwards from said base tank (30); supports (42) provided on floor of said base tank (30) for supporting said vertical elements (11); at least one planter pod (20a,20b,20c,20d,20e) slidably mounted on said vertical elements (11) and spaced apart from said base tank (30) and each other to form a vertical structure; openings (23) provided on floor of each of said planter pods (20a,20b,20c,20d,20e) for allowing passage of a fluid from the topmost planter pod (20a) to said base tank (30) via at least one intermittent planter pod (20b,20c,20d,20e) present. Thus, the system of the present disclosure facilitates elimination of vertical space as well as higher yield per horizontal space used.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. An aquaponics system (100) comprising: â¢ a base tank (30) supported on a floor surface; â¢ vertical elements (11) extending operatively upwards from said base tank (30); â¢ supports (42) provided on floor of said base tank (30) for supporting said vertical elements (11); â¢ at least one planter pod (20a,20b,20c,20d,20e) slidably mounted on said vertical elements (11) and spaced apart from said base tank (30) and each other to form a vertical structure; â¢ openings (23) provided on floor of each of said planter pods (20a,20b,20c,20d,20e) for allowing passage of a fluid from the topmost planter pod (20a) to said base tank (30) via at least one intermittent planter pod (20b,20c,20d,20e) present. 2. The aquaponics system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein a central vertical element (12) has a bottom end configured to receive said fluid contained in said base tank (30), and a top end configured to deliver said fluid into said topmost planter pod (20a). 3. The aquaponics system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein each of said planter pods (20a,20b,20c,20d,20e) are spaced non-uniformly to allow passage of light necessary for cultivation of crops. 4. The aquaponics system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein said aquaponics system (100) is made of a food-safe plastic material. 5. The aquaponics system (100) as claimed in claim 4, wherein said food-safe material is Acrylonitrile Butadiene Styrene (ABS). 6. The aquaponics system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein a pump is provided in said base tank (30) to transfer said fluid into said topmost planter pod (20a). 7. The aquaponics system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein ridges (41) are provided on surfaces of said base tank (30) to impart strength. 8. The aquaponics system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the edges (24) of said planter pods (20a,20b,20c,20d,20e) are rounded to provide a firm grip by manual hands. 9. The aquaponics system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein each of said planter pods (20a,20b,20c,20d,20e) and said base tank (30) has a frustum shaped profile.
